item.title: Gen Z in Nepal is not fucking around
item.description:
Edit: Fixed an annoying typo. I saw this meme and was like oh, must be a protest or something…..I was wrong, sooo very wrong
Most viral edit: Thanks for the accolades and upvotes, my dudes. I found a good article that explains in detail about the current situation in Nepal. You can read it in depth here (length warning): https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crkj0lzlr3ro But if you’re looking for the short version, this is from their overall coverage and a good summary: “What was the social media ban? Social media is a big part of Nepali life. Indeed, the country has one of South Asia's highest user rates per capita. The demonstrations were triggered by the government's decision last week to ban 26 social media platforms, including WhatsApp, Instagram and Facebook, for failing to meet a deadline to register with Nepal's ministry of communication and information technology. Critics accused the government of seeking to stifle an anti-corruption campaign with the ban, which was repealed on Monday night. While the ban was a catalyst for the current unrest, protesters are also channelling a more deep-rooted dissatisfaction with the country's authorities.” Original post below: They went for the gold, no fucking around with discussions and meetings, Gen Z was like fuck it, we’re burning down everything in this corrupt government. For additional context on what’s happening in Nepal: https://www.nytimes.com/live/2025/09/09/world/nepal-protests
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: In the winter of 1925,the town of Nome, Alaska, faced a terrifying diphtheria outbreak. With more than 10,000 lives at risk, especially those of children, the only antitoxin available was in Anchorage — nearly 1,000 miles away. Planes couldn’t fly
item.description: In the winter of 1925, the town of Nome, Alaska, faced a terrifying diphtheria outbreak. With more than 10,000 lives at risk, especially those of children, the only antitoxin available was in Anchorage — nearly 1,000 miles away. Planes couldn’t fly in the brutal cold, and the nearest railway ended 674 miles from Nome. The solution fell to an extraordinary relay of sled dogs and their mushers. Among them was a small but fierce Siberian Husky named Togo, leading the team of Leonhard Seppala. At 12 years old, Togo ran the longest and most treacherous stretch of the entire mission: 264 miles across storms, breaking sea ice, and the black Arctic night. While other teams averaged about 31 miles each, Togo and Seppala carried the serum through the most dangerous terrain — a shortcut across the frozen Norton Sound. Togo’s journey was made even more remarkable by his unlikely beginnings. Sickly as a pup and weighing just 48 pounds as an adult, he was once considered too small and weak for sled work. Given away as a pet, he escaped by leaping through a closed window to return to his kennel, determined to run. He earned his place in the team after breaking out again to follow Seppala’s sled on a training run, logging an astounding 75 miles on his very first day in harness. Togo’s courage changed history. In just five and a half days, the serum reached Nome, saving countless lives. Though Balto would become more famous for completing the final leg, it was Togo who endured the hardest, longest, and most perilous part of the relay. Decades later, his legacy was finally recognized. In 2011, Time named Togo the most heroic animal in history. In 2019, Disney released the film Togo starring Willem Dafoe as Seppala and Diesel, one of Togo’s own descendants, in the lead role. And in 2022, a statue of Togo was unveiled in Maine, where he spent his final years. Seppala himself said it best: “I thought of the ice and the darkness and the terrible wind and the irony that men could build planes and ships. But when Nome needed life in little packages of serum, it took the dogs to bring it through.”
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: BOROWITZ: Hand spurs
item.description: WASHINGTON (The Borowitz Report)—Appearing on Fox News Channel Monday night, Donald J. Trump said he was “furious” that an appeals court was forcing him to pay “all my hard-earned bribes” to the writer E. Jean Carroll. “For the last eight months, I’ve been working my ass off, shaking down universities, law firms, and media companies,” he told Sean Hannity. “And now I’m supposed to hand over all of those bribes to this lady, who hasn’t bribed a single person in her life?’ ”This should never be allowed to happen in this country,” he added. Turning to another controversy, Trump claimed he “could never have drawn” the just-released Epstein picture due to hand spurs.
item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.title: GovBrief Today #221
item.description: Republicans stole the Supreme Court in 2016. ⚖️ That's led to the overturning of abortion rights and handed presidents a roadmap for avoiding criminal immunity. Today, that broken court gave federal agents a green light to racially profile Americans, while the rest of the government is used to punish dissent and reward loyalty. The bill for a decade of cynical power plays has come due, and our rights are the currency. The time to stop this was before they seized the courts. The second-best time is today. Don't know what to do? Start local. Go to Mobilize and find actions in your area. Don’t leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/fema-whistleblower-claims [2] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-scotus-frozen-funding [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-vax-autism-post [4] https://go.govbrief.today/scotus-immigration-stop-frisk [5] https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-puerto-rico-venezuela [6] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-west-point-hanks [7] https://go.govbrief.today/carroll-lawsuit-verdict-upheld [8] https://go.govbrief.today/scotus-trump-fire-ftc [9] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-epstein-birthday-note [10] https://go.govbrief.today/routh-trump-attempt-trial [11] https://go.govbrief.today/doge-social-security-takeover [12] https://go.govbrief.today/texas-school-nurse-limits [13] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-domestic-violence-statistics [14] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-prayer-schools-protected [15] https://go.govbrief.today/adams-rikers-ice-blocked [16] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-navarro-emails-dropped [17] https://go.govbrief.today/white-house-vigil-persists Lil' reminder that a gift from the site's owners is that I'm not allowed to use chat and I'm not allowed to know why. So if that's you out there, feel free to email me at the address on the website or reply to the substack email. I'm the only one reading both.
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos

item.dc:creator: inkican

item.title: Silverheels was the first Indigenous Native American to be commemorated on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.
item.description: Details about his star: Location: 6538 Hollywood Blvd. Awarded: July 17, 1979. Category: Television. Significance: Silverheels was the first Indigenous Native American to be commemorated on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. About Jay Silverheels: Born: Harold J. Smith on May 26, 1912, on the Six Nations Reserve in Ontario, Canada. Career: A talented athlete, he excelled at lacrosse before becoming an actor, known for his role as Tonto. Legacy: He worked to improve the portrayal of Native Americans in film and television. Death: He passed away in 1980 from a stroke, but his star remains a lasting tribute to his career.
item.dc:creator: Ice2Ice2

item.title: some drawings i did about water
item.description:
…
. . .
. . . .
. .
i got ill recently and water was a big theme: hydrating, things “flowing”, water as healing, so while healing up i drew these images.
(bonus: wizard) (with cat) edit: cool, front page, thanks. if you’re unfamiliar with my work (usually comics) i did a post introducing myself last week: https://imgur.com/gallery/i-never-introduced-myself-to-imgur-06mFw9x
item.dc:creator: owencomicsdotcom
